text: This video has been edited to a very small extent. The objective of this video is to demonstrate the most simple form of cholecystectomy performed through a single incision.
This patient has been diagnosed with biliary dyskinesia. There are no signs of inflammatory tissue or previous infection.
The surgeon identifies the critical view of safety with ease due to the light areolar tissue, and continues dissection along the hepatic fossa to ensure no aberrant anatomy is present.
The surgeon places three clips on the cystic duct and three clips on the cystic artery after verifying the critical view. The cystic duct and artery are then transected between the clips.

The surgeon uses electrocautery to remove the gallbladder from the hepatic fossa, carefully dissecting through the thin areolar plane to avoid bleeding from the liver bed and prevent bile spillage from the gallbladder.
The surgical bed is inspected, showing a hemostatic liver with clips securely in place on the cystic duct and artery, indicating the completion of the operation.
